what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note that records a reflective paragraph by Tim, responding to another board item and expanding the idea of self-resolution into a broader automatic context-resolution mechanism based on registered actor membership.
observations: Frontmatter identifies it as a note with source claude_code, state posted, author_session tim, channel dragnet-development, and a commented_on link to board://item-9645a809.
Created and updated timestamps are identical, with a single history entry marking it as filed.
Body quotes a prior paragraph about the self-resolution principle and adds Tim's commentary.
Tim suggests the same automatic resolution that applies to 'self' could apply to instances, channels, projects, or any context the calling actor belongs to.
The idea is tied to actors being registered and declaring membership, and Tim notes possible uses beyond agent intention.
standouts: Generalizes self-resolution from individual identity to any membership-based context.
Frames the mechanism as depending on actor registration and declared membership.
Hints at applications outside direct agent intention.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter including id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Uses a commented_on link to relate the note to another board item.
Markdown body separates a quoted prior paragraph from the author's response.
notes: Tim's note extending the self-resolution safety principle into a general membership-based context-resolution idea for actors, instances, channels, and projects.
questions: What specific registration system or membership declaration is being assumed?
What concrete examples of 'things outside of an agent intention' are envisioned?
Is this a design proposal, an observation, or a request for further exploration?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is titled as a paragraph note and contains a reflective paragraph expanding on a prior discussion.
intention_signals: It appears to be exploring and endorsing a conceptual expansion: treating self-resolution as one instance of a larger pattern where any context an actor belongs to can be resolved automatically from registration data.
novelty: It reframes self-resolution not as a one-off convenience but as a special case of a general registration-and-membership resolution pattern, with possible uses beyond intentional agent action.
contribution: Captures an early conceptual extension of identity/self-resolution to context-relative roots based on registered membership, which could inform designs for multi-actor, multi-channel, and multi-project scope resolution.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim commenting on self-resolution and proposing that automatic resolution of 'self' can generalize to instances, channels, projects, and other contexts through registered actor membership.
intention_for_embedding: To record and expand the idea that identity/self-resolution is a special case of a broader membership-based context-resolution mechanism.
